title: v3.0.5 Patch Notes
body:
CHANGELOG:

Parts of the UI are being migrated to a newer format. This should make the app more performant and quicker to load, especially on older devices. This may cause a small delay (or even a temporary blank screen) the first time you navigate to the view, after which it should not happen again. This may occur one time per view for the next few updates as more of the UI is ported.


  • Syncing has been significantly upgraded. Please see https://github.com/orgs/massif-press/discussions/204 for further details
  • The non-interaction bug that occurred on Safari with high resolution displays has been resolved
  • The Waterfox idempotency key issue has been resolved
  • The NPC stat editing interface has been improved, and will now highlight edited stats and prompt you to keep, clear, or project stat changes on tier change
  • Bottom nav bars have been reoriented for devices with large bottom bevels (such as the iPhone)
  • Pilot Bond Powers now list by power type, rather than source
  • Pilot Gear now correctly displays effect text
  • Limited charge/usage issues in Active Mode have been resolved
  • Multiple v2 import issues have been resolved (more of this coming soon)
  • More v2 LCPs will now correctly show Reaction trigger text
  • Multiple minor display issues have been cleaned up

v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app


Please submit bug tickets and feature requests at the COMP/CON GitHub repo

title: v3.0.4 Patch Notes
body:
CHANGELOG:

  • Drag-and-drop has been re-implemented in the Pilot Roster, Mech Systems list, NPC Features list, and Encounter combatant list.
  • For mobile users, tapping and holding a pilot roster component for 3 seconds will initiate the re-ordering/drag and drop mode
  • Pilot Mechs are now visible on Pilot Roster list items
  • Mechs can be favorited in the Mech Hangar or from the Mech Sheet (star button) - this re-implements the Active Mech UI in v3. Favorite mechs are set as default selections for Active Mode
  • Fixed a number of Firefox-specific rendering issues, including weapon mounts disappearing with multiple mods/core bonuses
  • Fixed mobile share code input not accepting pastes/not auto-incrementing inputs
  • Cleaned up some miscellaneous Compendium item rendering issues
  • Fixed Caltrop Launcher missing Effect text
  • Corrected Lesson of the Open Door to use the non-action proxy
  • Fixed missing trigger text on v2 homebrew LCPs
  • v2 LCP NPC features will now always start expanded in Active Mode
  • Base/Optional features are now differentiated in the NPC feature selector
  • Users can save custom environments and sitreps as user data presets
  • More flexibility in selecting/removing Bond Powers
  • NPC Scan statblocks can now be generated with full feature info
  • Bugged Drone/Deployable activations not costing their actions has been fixed
  • Several compendium filtering issues have been fixed

v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app


Please submit bug tickets and feature requests at the COMP/CON GitHub repo


KNOWN ISSUES:

  • The Safari browser, at certain resolutions, will prevent interaction will the right side of the C/C interface. This is still being investigated.
  • The Waterfox browser automatically generates an idempotency key by default that breaks many cc-backend functions. Consider using another browser or disabling experimental setting network.http.idempotencyKey. If you've enabled this on Firefox it will have to be disabled as well (but it shouldn't be on by default)

title: v3.0.3 Patch Notes
body:
CHANGELOG:

  • Fixed some remaining damage calculation issues
  • Added a "None" activation proxy to better comport to Lancer rules
  • Added LCPs the ability to assign NPC Classes and Templates features from other sources
  • Corrected multiple data errors in CRB and other first-party LCP data
  • Fixed various display, rendering, and alignment bugs
  • Added sort functions to the GM Label interface
  • Improved performance in many views (more of this to come)
  • Added the ability to select LCP display in the compendium and other cc-browser elements by LCP Configurations
  • Added missing item types to the Compendium search
  • Updated Local PC Active Mode attack interface to no longer require an enemy defense target value
  • Fixed the missing group rename interface

v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app


Please submit bug tickets and feature requests at the COMP/CON GitHub repo


KNOWN ISSUES:

  • The Safari browser, at certain resolutions, will prevent interaction will the right side of the C/C interface. This is still being investigated.
  • The Waterfox browser automatically generates an idempotency key by default that breaks many cc-backend functions. Consider using another browser or disabling experimental setting network.http.idempotencyKey. If you've enabled this on Firefox it will have to be disabled as well (but it shouldn't be on by default)

title: Migration Repair Tool Available
body:
An experimental migration repair tool is now available in the options menu. This tool can automatically fix certain common issues with v2 data migration, but has not yet been thoroughly tested. It is strongly recommended to make a backup before applying fixes.

If you encounter any issues with the tool or have suggestions for additional fixes to add, please submit an issue at the GitHub repo.

title: v3.0.2 Patch Notes
body:
CHANGELOG:

  • Fixed Massif LCP itch links
  • Fixed various text alignment and rendering issues
  • Fixed various v2 legacy export issues
  • Statuses/Conditions are now labeled in mobile pilot sheet views
  • Compendium browser table can now be multi-sorted
  • Fixed missing innate accuracy/difficulty stat in NPC weapons
  • NPC acc/diff and attack bonuses now correctly apply to attacks in Active Mode
  • Fixed various table sorting issues
  • Fixed a migration issue on mobile devices
  • Various data updates and fixes (more coming soon)

v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app

KNOWN ISSUES:

  • The Waterfox browser automatically generates an idempotency key by default that breaks many cc-backend functions. Consider using another browser or disabling experimental setting network.http.idempotencyKey. If you've enabled this on Firefox it will have to be disabled as well (but it shouldn't be on by default)

  • Most data loading issues have been addressed, but some v2 data can migrate into a bad state. If NPCs aren't populating encounters or they don't have any features in the Compendium please remove and reinstall the NPC LCPs. Sometimes this can happen with Core Bonuses, and reinstalling the offending LCP works here as well

  • Some older v2 imports migrate with ambiguous flavor text values that v3 tries to preserve. This could mean full compendium text appears on items in eg. the mech sheet and the encounter runner. This text can be removed by clearing the items' custom flavor text, or removing and re-adding the item.

title: v3.0.1 Patch Notes
body:
CHANGELOG:

  • Many, many bugs and edge cases have been addressed, thanks to user reports. If something isn't working right, please check the issue tracker and submit a ticket if your bug isn't on there.

  • Some share code imports and share pages were getting erroneously pushed. This has been corrected and should no longer happen.

  • Importing pilot groups could re-list pilots multiple times in the roster. This has been corrected and the duplicates will no longer appear.

  • The button to reset the v2 migration process now correctly appears in the options menu if dismissed.


v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app

KNOWN ISSUES:

  • The Waterfox browser automatically generates an idempotency key by default that breaks many cc-backend functions. Consider using another browser or disabling experimental setting network.http.idempotencyKey. If you've enabled this on Firefox it will have to be disabled as well (but it shouldn't be on by default)

  • Most data loading issues have been addressed, but some v2 data can migrate into a bad state. If NPCs aren't populating encounters or they don't have any features in the Compendium please remove and reinstall the NPC LCPs. Sometimes this can happen with Core Bonuses, and reinstalling the offending LCP works here as well

  • Some older v2 imports migrate with ambiguous flavor text values that v3 tries to preserve. This could mean full compendium text appears on items in eg. the mech sheet and the encounter runner. This text can be removed by clearing the items' custom flavor text, or removing and re-adding the item.

title: COMP/CON v3 Launch
body:
v3.0.0 is live! Your data should have been migrated automatically. A full migration guide can be found here.

CHANGELOG:

Too many changes to list. Major details can be found here.
Please report any bugs after checking the task list to see if it has already been reported or is resolved and pending deployment.

v3.0.x task list: https://github.com/orgs/massif-press/projects/4/views/7

v2.3.21a is available at https://old.compcon.app

KNOWN ISSUES:

  • The Waterfox browser automatically generates an idempotency key by default that breaks many cc-backend functions. Consider using another browser or disabling experimental setting network.http.idempotencyKey. If you've enabled this on Firefox it will have to be disabled as well (but it shouldn't be on by default)

  • Certain data-heavy operations might lag on slower devices, especially with many LCPs loaded. Expect performance improvements over the coming weeks.

  • v3 data Foundry imports and other VTTs/external tools should work if you use the v2 legacy export options. The Lancer Foundry Module team is currently working on incorporating new v3 formats and backend changes. Currently v3 cloud data won't sync to external tools.

  • Automated Stress/Structure rolls are not yet implemented in v3.

  • All v2 LCPs should work but will not be furnished with the new v3 active mode features. If a v2 LCP is broken or missing data after migration, try removing and reinstalling it. If that still doesn't work.

  • There are a few COMP/CON-isms in terms of how v3 "renders" certain Lancer rules and edge cases that may be odd/inaccurate/less than ideal. These are all still very valid candidates for edits and adjustments and if you notice something that doesn't sit right please consider opening a bug ticket or feature request.
